title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, from Lynnfield, runs the Boston Marathon supporting Mass General after losing his second mother to cancer.
Sharla Sieve, Mallett's second mother, passed from Stage 4 metastatic colorectal cancer, inspiring his marathon participation and fundraising.
Mallett, a videographer for the Boston Bruins, runs marathons honoring Sieve and supporting healthcare innovations at MGH.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect the Patriots to acquire Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown after June 1 due to salary-cap implications.
The Eagles' dead cap hit for trading A.J. Brown would decrease from $43 million to $16.4 million if traded after June 1.
The Eagles reportedly seek a first-round draft pick and more in exchange for Brown, a three-time Pro Bowler with six 1,000-yard seasons.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Amanda Seyfried stars in 'The Testament of Ann Lee,' now streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' is available on Netflix, with Ralph Fiennes in a notable role.
Ken Burns' documentary on Henry David Thoreau is streaming on PBS with Jeff Goldblum as Thoreau.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox defeated the Padres 5-2 at Fenway Park, ending a five-game losing streak.
Caleb Durbin delivered an RBI single, ending a personal 0-for-34 slump dating back to last season.
Willson Contreras hit his first home run with the team, contributing to the 6th-inning lead surge.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots start phase one of offseason workouts on April 20 with conditioning drills and meetings.
OTAs for the Patriots are scheduled on May 27, May 29, June 1-2, June 4, June 8-9, and June 11.
Patriots' mandatory minicamp takes place from June 15-17, concluding the voluntary offseason program.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots are projected to draft edge rushers Cashius Howell and Gabe Jacas in the first two rounds.
Howell, from Texas A&M, recorded 11.5 sacks last season and is likened to Will McDonald IV by NFL analysts.
Jacas of Illinois is noted for his strong run defense and is compared to Matthew Judon by NFL experts.

title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox defeated the San Diego Padres 5-2 at Fenway Park, signaling a fresh start after a 1-5 season start.
Sonny Gray pitched six strong innings, and Willson Contreras hit a pivotal home run over the Green Monster.
Caleb Durbin ended a hitless streak with an RBI single, reflecting the team's potential for recovery and improvement.

title: North Andover police working to get body cameras after Kelsey Fitzsimmons shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover plans to implement a body-worn camera program for police, seeking grant funding to reduce taxpayer costs.
The initiative follows an incident where officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons was shot by a colleague during a restraining order delivery.
A judge highlighted the absence of body cameras during Fitzsimmons' trial, emphasizing it as a factor in the verdict.

title: Xander Bogaerts talks Roman Anthony, pressure from fans in his return to Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Xander Bogaerts returned to Fenway Park, now plays for the San Diego Padres after signing an 11-year, $280 million contract.
Bogaerts received a standing ovation from the Fenway Park crowd as he returned as a visitor on Opening Day.
He acknowledges high expectations in Boston and sees 21-year-old Roman Anthony as a key player for the Red Sox.

title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Visitors can explore Boston's Freedom Trail, a 2.5-mile path connecting 16 historical sites, attracting over 4 million annually.
Six Flags New England debuts its Quantum Accelerator coaster on April 11 for season pass holders and April 17 for the public.
Portsmouth, N.H., hosts Restaurant Week from April 9-18, with 30+ venues offering prix fixe menus priced at $32, $42, and $52.
